engagement.justification: The query is opaque (an analogy-type label), but the response gestures at a thing that 'works for all' being used to prevent a small bad thing, which loosely tracks an allegory/heuristic theme. It touches a topic without engaging a clear central ask.

coherence.justification: Steps are loosely chained with an objection-and-reply shape, but several transitions ('it does work even if no one looks') are non-sequiturs and the referent of 'the thing' stays vague throughout.

substance.justification: It introduces an objection (the thing works unobserved) and weighs it, which is a real move, but the content stays abstract and never develops a concrete case or distinction.

expressive_adequacy.justification: The point is recoverable but heavily obscured by 'the thing'/'a thing that works for all' circumlocutions that never resolve into a clear referent.

engagement.justification: The query concerns an indicative conditional / modus tollens about a criminal; the response is the boilerplate loss-aversion essay and addresses nothing in the prompt.

coherence.justification: Internally the loss-aversion mini-argument reads cleanly, but it is a self-contained template with no link to the question.

substance.justification: It asserts a loss-aversion claim but cites no case and pushes nothing; it is canned paraphrase of a psychology truism unrelated to the prompt.

expressive_adequacy.justification: The loss-aversion content itself comes through clearly in UGF, but it is the wrong content for this prompt.

engagement.justification: The query asks about group cooperation that costs you but helps everyone; the response directly addresses sharing the work so 'we all win,' engaging the central tension.

coherence.justification: The opening fragment ('the same means you lose some thing and you get more') is garbled, but the cooperation-through-sharing chain is mostly followable.

substance.justification: It identifies the give-up-for-mutual-benefit move and the role of others reciprocating, but does not develop a counterexample or push past the basic point.

expressive_adequacy.justification: The cooperative point is recoverable, but the broken first sentence and terse phrasing make it less than clean.

engagement.justification: Query is an explanatory-analogy epistemology label; the response is the boilerplate loss-aversion essay, unrelated to the prompt.

coherence.justification: It opens with a stray period and otherwise reads as the canned loss-aversion chain; internally simple but disconnected from any prompt.

substance.justification: Bare loss-aversion claim with no case or development; pure off-topic paraphrase.

expressive_adequacy.justification: The (wrong) content is expressed clearly enough in UGF.

engagement.justification: The query asks why sharing all you know is not always best; the response addresses holding back knowledge to stay safe and keep advantage, engaging the central question.

coherence.justification: It moves through reasons for and against sharing, but several steps wander (teaching wall-building, 'feeling sure and working hard together') and the through-line is loose.

substance.justification: It surfaces multiple real considerations: losing competitive edge, secrets meant for few, signaling unreadiness for trust, and the value of selective disclosure.

expressive_adequacy.justification: Largely clear UGF; only minor clunk ('knowing-how') with the argument coming through well.

coherence.justification: The 'pieces of a game' fitting metaphor is consistent, but steps are choppy and the closing 'The case is good' is an unearned, abrupt conclusion.

substance.justification: It identifies coherence-of-parts and that one weak piece breaks the whole, a relevant move, but never develops or illustrates it.

expressive_adequacy.justification: The structural point about fitting pieces comes through reasonably clearly in UGF.

engagement.justification: The query asks how we build a picture of the world from small pieces of what we see; the response directly explains accumulating and joining perceptual pieces into a whole.

coherence.justification: Mostly followable, but one step is self-undermining ('a thing that looks like a tree... our mind says it is a house'), a sloppy contradiction in the example.

substance.justification: It identifies pattern-matching to prior stored cases and synthesis into a whole, a relevant mechanism, but stays at the descriptive level without a sharper distinction.

engagement.justification: Query asks what it means for a case to be put together well; the response drifts into incoherent talk of songs, drawings, and 'the maker did the work,' barely touching argument quality.

coherence.justification: Largely disconnected fragments; the song/picture/words framing never connects to a claim about a well-built case.

engagement.justification: The query asks what makes someone happy in the deepest way; the response addresses lasting happiness from close relationships and meaningful shared work versus fleeting wins.

coherence.justification: A clear progression from short-lived wins to durable bonds and competence; steps mostly follow, with only minor looseness.

substance.justification: It distinguishes momentary pleasure from durable well-being and ties the latter to relationships and mastery that compound over time, real work beyond paraphrase.

expressive_adequacy.justification: The eudaimonia-style point comes through clearly in UGF.

engagement.justification: Query is a counterexample to a resemblance theory of pictorial representation; the response rambles about 'books,' 'the book way,' and an 'old man,' never addressing depiction or resemblance.

coherence.justification: Largely disconnected and circular ('the book way needs the book ways to be true'); the argument never resolves into a coherent claim.

substance.justification: No real engagement with representation or a counterexample; mostly empty self-referential talk about 'the book way.'

expressive_adequacy.justification: Restricted vocabulary and the opaque 'book way' coinage break the argument; the point does not come through.

engagement.justification: The query asks how to anticipate what the other person will do before acting; the response touches strategic anticipation but drifts into self-preservation and harm, missing the central ask.

coherence.justification: Steps are disjointed and partly self-contradictory ('the other person has no power left in their body and mind, so they might get hurt or die'), with a confused close.

substance.justification: It does name strategic anticipation and the tension between helping others and protecting oneself, but the development is muddled rather than substantive.

expressive_adequacy.justification: The strategic-anticipation point is partly recoverable but obscured by the confused harm/death tangent.

engagement.justification: The query asks what makes a good reason for believing something; the response directly addresses coherence with prior knowledge, reliable sources, convergence of viewpoints, and avoiding confirmation bias.

coherence.justification: A well-structured enumerated argument (source reliability, convergence, motive-skepticism, echo chambers) that mostly follows, with only minor looseness.

substance.justification: It surfaces several genuine epistemic criteria, including fit with existing knowledge, multiple-perspective convergence, and the danger of only hearing agreement, real work.

expressive_adequacy.justification: The epistemology comes through clearly in UGF despite some wordy phrasing.

engagement.justification: The query asks why consequences matter for whether an act was right; the response contrasts judging the act itself versus judging the resulting state of the world, engaging the consequentialism question.

coherence.justification: The money/food worked example is badly tangled, with contradictory claims about who has too much or too little, making the chain hard to follow.

substance.justification: It draws a real distinction between act-focused and end-state-focused evaluation and attempts a worked case, genuine philosophical effort even though the case misfires.

expressive_adequacy.justification: The act-vs-consequence distinction is recoverable, but the confused example and UGF phrasing obscure much of the reasoning.
